# Policy Bundle Loader Evidence

<!-- Generated by go run ./tools/policybundleevidence -write-doc. Do not edit by hand. -->

[Back to invariants](../invariants.md)

Executable SSOT: [`policy-bundle-loader.riido.json`](policy-bundle-loader.riido.json).

Evidence artifact: `policy-bundle-loader-evidence`.

Prove the C7 policy bundle loader shape, fail-closed validation, daemon env wiring, and pure policy boundary from local source evidence.

## Implemented Facts

| Fact | Summary | Evidence |
| --- | --- | --- |
| bundle file path is daemon env wiring | RIIDO_POLICY_BUNDLE_PATH is read by cmd/riido and passed to policy.LoadPolicyBundleFile. | `env-path-constant`, `daemon-load-policy-file` |
| file version becomes active version | A loaded bundle file sets daemon settings PolicyBundle and PolicyBundleDoc version. | `daemon-file-version-test`, `daemon-version-mismatch-test` |
| no path uses local default | No bundle path uses policy-bundle.local.v0 and allows only the Claude audit native config hook. | `default-version-constant`, `default-bundle-source`, `default-bundle-test` |
| version env without path only retags built-in default | RIIDO_POLICY_BUNDLE_VERSION without a file path changes the default bundle version tag only. | `daemon-version-retag-source` |
| unknown JSON fields fail closed | Policy bundle parsing disallows unknown fields and trailing JSON values. | `disallow-unknown-fields`, `trailing-json-reject`, `unknown-field-test` |
| minimal execution subset | The executable bundle supports unsafe_bypass, native_config_hooks, native_config_files, and tool_use only. | `allowed-surface-set` |
| schema and required fields | schema_version, version, effective_since, and trust_tier_policies are validated before use. | `schema-version-constant`, `schema-version-validate`, `version-required`, `effective-since-required`, `policies-required` |
| superseded ordering | superseded_at must be after effective_since when present. | `superseded-after-effective` |
| known trust tiers only | trust_tier_policies keys must be known trust tier enum values. | `unknown-tier-validate` |
| unsafe bypass constraints | Unsafe bypass surfaces must be known, unique, and not allowed on Host or Unknown. | `unsafe-known-switch`, `unsafe-duplicate`, `unsafe-host-unknown`, `unsafe-host-test` |
| native config hook constraints | Native config hook surfaces must be known, unique, and not allowed on Unknown. | `native-hook-known-switch`, `native-hook-duplicate`, `native-hook-unknown`, `native-hook-unknown-test` |
| native config file constraints | Native config file surfaces must be known, unique, and not allowed on Unknown. | `native-file-known-switch`, `native-file-duplicate`, `native-file-unknown`, `native-file-unknown-test` |
| tool-use constraints | Tool-use risk surfaces must be known, unique, and not allowed on Unknown. | `tool-use-known-switch`, `tool-use-duplicate`, `tool-use-unknown`, `tool-use-unknown-test` |

## Boundaries

| Boundary | Owner | Summary |
| --- | --- | --- |
| policy parser purity | internal/policy | The parser validates bytes and files; daemon env interpretation remains in cmd/riido. |
| policy decision execution | C4/C5/C6 runtime consumers | The loader owns bundle validation, not provider process, scheduling, or workspace mutation execution. |
| future C7 policy scope | security SSOT | The loader only accepts the minimal executable subset listed in this manifest. |

## Forbidden Surfaces

| Surface | Reason |
| --- | --- |
| `policy parser direct env ownership` | daemon env parsing belongs to cmd/riido, not the pure policy package |
| `policy loader remote fetch` | current physical policy bundle form is a local JSON file |

## Assertions

- `Policy bundle loader reader doc must be generated from this manifest.`
- `Every implemented loader claim must point at local source evidence.`
- `internal/policy must stay free of daemon env parsing and remote bundle fetch ownership.`
